mechanism_narrative: The 3-month change in the federal funds rate negatively predicts S&P 500 20-day returns, with lower or unchanged rates supporting positive equity performance through discount rate compression.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market overheating that tightens wage costs and compresses profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines > 0.3pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based demand expansion that drives corporate revenue acceleration; the joint condition produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond either variable alone.
failure_condition: The joint model using RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform the best univariate proxy by more than 0.01 in R-squared over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During pre-tightening cycles, an acceleration in consumer price inflation pressures the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates, increasing equity discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FOMC expected to raise rates December 2015
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations during pre-tightening cycles with CPIAUCSL acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal contracting manufacturing activity and weakening corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth exceeding 200,000 jobs per month on average over three months combined with an unemployment rate below 5.5% signals robust labor income growth that supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month avg monthly change > 200k and UNRATE < 5.5%
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% quarter-over-quarter annualized signals demand-driven earnings expansion that lifts equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1% signals weakening consumer demand that translates into lower corporate revenues and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month.
